expectations.text: Output contains an explicit rating on a defined scale
expectations.evidence: 'Advance to hiring manager call' — recommendation but no numeric scale or structured rating

expectations.text: Strengths section with specific evidence (500k user growth, fintech domain match)
expectations.evidence: 6 strengths: domain match, 10x growth, portfolio skills, agency scrappiness, self-directed learning, public body of work
No distinct gaps section — gaps are embedded within the 'Risks and How to Probe Them' section

expectations.text: Risks section exists and is distinct from bias flags
expectations.evidence: 4 risks (US/EU experience, self-taught, agency vs product, iteration speed) with probe questions for each
'Bottom Line' summary but no structured conditional framework (fit if X, not fit if Y, what changes your mind)
6 questions, mix of behavioral and situational

expectations.text: Includes a scoring rubric for the call
expectations.evidence: No scoring rubric. Has a call structure and questions but no rubric for evaluating responses.

expectations.text: Questions target actual unknowns, not proxy credentials
expectations.evidence: Questions target working style, scrappiness, collaboration, and iteration speed — not credentials

expectations.text: Explicitly flags 'no US/EU experience' as potential bias
expectations.evidence: Bias Check section: 'This is a geographic proxy, not a skill assessment'

expectations.text: Explicitly flags 'self-taught / no degree' as potential bias
expectations.evidence: Bias Check section: 'This is a credentials proxy' — recommends not letting it influence decision
